edges.evidence_quote: On 14 May 2016, three people were seriously injured as a result of a shootout between the Hells Angels and Los Tehuelches, a rival motorcycle gang with Neo-Nazi sympathies consisting primarily of active and former police officers, at a highway gas station in Luján, Buenos Aires.
edges.context: The two clubs engaged in a shootout at a gas station in Argentina.

edges.evidence_quote: The previous day, authorities in Saxony had offered a €10,000 reward for information on Matz, who was wanted in relation to a shooting in Leipzig on 25 June 2016 in which a member of the United Tribuns gang was killed and two other men were critically injured.
edges.context: A Hells Angels member was wanted for a shooting that killed a United Tribuns member.

edges.evidence_quote: On October 4, 2009, several Hells Angels and allied Red Devils performed a raid on an Outlaws clubhouse in Kortrijk.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Red Devils jointly attacked an Outlaws clubhouse.

edges.evidence_quote: On October 4, 2009, several Hells Angels and allied Red Devils performed a raid on an Outlaws clubhouse in Kortrijk.
edges.context: Hells Angels raided an Outlaws clubhouse, part of a territorial dispute.

edges.evidence_quote: On October 4, 2009, several Hells Angels and allied Red Devils performed a raid on an Outlaws clubhouse in Kortrijk.
edges.context: Red Devils participated in a raid on Outlaws clubhouse alongside Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: The two clubs would wage war against each other between September 1983 and December 1985.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Bullshit MC fought a war in Copenhagen.

edges.evidence_quote: Carsten Bresløv (born June 9, 1958), a member of the Morticians who were a club affiliated with the Hells Angels at the time, entered the store wearing a mask and shot Blåbjerg.
edges.context: Morticians were affiliated with Hells Angels and carried out a murder on their behalf.

edges.evidence_quote: Anker Walther "Høvding" Marcus (January 17, 1947 – December 21, 1985) was then murdered by Ole Bonnesen Nielsen and Rene Nøddeskov Ludvigsen, two members of the Black Sheep (another Hells Angels prospect club), following a Christmas party at Nemoland Café in Christiania on December 21, 1985.
edges.context: Black Sheep were a Hells Angels prospect club that carried out a murder.

edges.evidence_quote: This eventually led to the Great Nordic Biker War, a conflict over control of the drug trade between the two most powerful outlaw biker gangs in Scandinavia, the Hells Angels and the Bandidos.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Bandidos fought a war over drug trade control in Scandinavia.

edges.evidence_quote: In April and May 1996, the clubhouse of a Hells Angels prospect club, Avengers MC, was attacked in Aalborg.
edges.context: Avengers MC was a Hells Angels prospect club whose clubhouse was attacked.

edges.evidence_quote: Outlaws president Thore "Henki" Holm and a French Outlaws member were subsequently shot and wounded by a member of the Untouchables MC, a Hells Angels ally.
edges.context: Untouchables MC was an ally of Hells Angels that attacked Outlaws members.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2007, a Hells Angels-associated gang named Altid Klar-81... was formed in Denmark to combat immigrant street gangs
edges.context: AK81 is a support/supplementary gang associated with Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: A turf war between the Hells Angels and the Black Army on Funen led to a number of clashes
edges.context: Hells Angels and Black Army were in a turf war involving clashes and a grenade attack.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels became involved in a war with the Loyal to Familia (LTF) gang to monopolize the cannabis trade in Christiania.
edges.context: Hells Angels and LTF were in a war over cannabis trade monopoly.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels went to war with the Bandidos when after first Bandidos chapter in Europe was opened in Marseille in August 1989.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Bandidos were in a war starting in 1989, with multiple violent incidents.

edges.evidence_quote: The Filthy Rebels, a Hells Angels-affiliated gang in Grenoble... were subsequently 'patched over' by the Hells Angels on 7 September 1991 to become the club's Grenoble chapter.
edges.context: Filthy Rebels were a Hells Angels-affiliated gang that later became a Hells Angels chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: police, acting on intelligence supplied by an informant in the Buccaneers, another motorcycle gang affiliated with the Hells Angels
edges.context: Buccaneers were a motorcycle gang affiliated with Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: On 18 April 1999, the Hells Angels absorbed the RMB ('Raving Mad Brothers') biker gang in Gagny
edges.context: Hells Angels absorbed RMB biker gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The first German charter of the Hells Angels was founded in Hamburg via a 'patch over' of the Bloody Devils biker gang in March 1973
edges.context: Bloody Devils were patched over to become Hells Angels chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: The other Hells Angels members and 250 of 497 members of the motorcycle club 'Bones' in Hannover under its President Frank Hanebuth, took over the power in the Hamburg Kiez
edges.context: Members of Bones joined Hells Angels in taking over Hamburg Kiez.

edges.evidence_quote: The perpetrators were members of the Chicanos, a support group of the Bandidos motorcycle gang.
edges.context: Chicanos are a support group of Bandidos.

edges.evidence_quote: They were accused, along with another Hells Angels member... of having allegedly murdered the 45-year-old President of the Donnersberg Outlaws MC in June 2009.
edges.context: Hells Angels members allegedly murdered Outlaws MC president.

edges.evidence_quote: In January 2010, the President of the Flensburg Hells Angels was arrested, accused of attempted homicide and hit-and-run driving, by having hit a Bandidos member with his car on the A7, reports say.
edges.context: Hells Angels member hit a Bandidos member with his car, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In February 2010 in Potsdam, about 70 supporters of the Berlin chapter of the Bandidos MC, who usually are hostile to the Hells Angels, moved to the Berlin chapter of the Hells Angels.
edges.context: Bandidos supporters defected to Hells Angels, indicating a shift in allegiance but underlying hostility.

edges.evidence_quote: On March 15, 2010, a 21-year-old supporter of the Bandidos was stabbed and badly injured in Kiel.
edges.context: A Bandidos supporter was stabbed, likely by Hells Angels, indicating ongoing conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On March 17, 2010, a Bonn Hells Angels member shot dead a 42-year-old police officer of the SEK during a house search. He was subsequently acquitted of murder charges by the German Supreme court, stating that he acted in self-defense after murder threats by Bandido members.
edges.context: Hells Angels member claimed self-defense against Bandidos threats, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Since March 2010, a Hells Angels member has been standing trial in Duisburg for having murdered an Oberhausen Bandidos member in Hochfeld, Duisburg on October 8, 2009, who was executed with a headshot in its red-light district.
edges.context: Hells Angels member murdered a Bandidos member, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The support club Red Devils MC Cologne was also banned.
edges.context: Red Devils MC is a support club for Hells Angels, a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: The Fáfnir biker gang began 'prospecting' for the Hells Angels in August 2009, shortly after four of its members were arrested in a ISK 50 million fraud case. Fáfnir became an official HAMC support club during a visit to a Hells Angels event in England.
edges.context: Fáfnir became a support club for Hells Angels, a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2011, Fáfnir 'patched over' to the Hells Angels at a ceremony in Oslo, Norway.
edges.context: Fáfnir merged into Hells Angels, a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: Twenty-three bikers were arrested following a fight between Hells Angels and Mongols, in which several gunshots were fired and one person wounded, at the Van der Valk hotel in Rotterdam on April 7, 2016.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Mongols fought, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: New Zealand's Hells Angels are allied with the Head Hunters and the Road Knights.
edges.context: Hells Angels are allied with Head Hunters, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: New Zealand's Hells Angels are allied with the Head Hunters and the Road Knights.
edges.context: Hells Angels are allied with Road Knights, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: Seven Hells Angels received prison sentences of up to ten years for their part in the murder of Bradley Earl Haora, a nineteen-year-old Highway 61 member killed with a shotgun in Mount Eden on 29 December 1975.
edges.context: Hells Angels murdered a Highway 61 member, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 1971, members of the Hells Angels, Highway 61, the Mongrel Mob and the Polynesian Panthers were involved in a large-scale brawl in Auckland, which resulted in numerous arrests.
edges.context: Hells Angels brawled with Mongrel Mob, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 1971, members of the Hells Angels, Highway 61, the Mongrel Mob and the Polynesian Panthers were involved in a large-scale brawl in Auckland, which resulted in numerous arrests.
edges.context: Hells Angels brawled with Polynesian Panthers, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels 'patched over' the Lost Breed in Nelson in 2016
edges.context: Hells Angels absorbed Lost Breed, a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: and the Mothers MC in Palmerston North in 2018
edges.context: Hells Angels absorbed Mothers MC, a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2018 Portuguese authorities publicly declared that they found strong evidence of potential gang violence events against Bandidos, a rival motorcycle club, specifically against Mário Machado, once the leader of the Portuguese Hammerskins, who is suspected to be a member of the latter.
edges.context: Hells Angels targeted Bandidos in Portugal, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels became associated with the Johannesburg "bouncer mafia", a criminal network involved in narcotics trafficking and extortion in the city's nightclub industry that emerged in the late apartheid era.
edges.context: Hells Angels associated with the Johannesburg bouncer mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Paul Witworth, a British national who allegedly maintained links with the Clerkenwell crime syndicate of London.
edges.context: A Hells Angels associate maintained links with the Clerkenwell crime syndicate.

edges.evidence_quote: On March 3, 1998, the vice president of the Hirdmen Motorcycle Club in Stockholm was killed by several gunshots to the neck. ... The investigation alleged the Hells Angels milieu as perpetrators, although no one was convicted.
edges.context: Hells Angels were alleged perpetrators in the murder of Hirdmen MC's vice president.

edges.evidence_quote: A former member of Rodents Motorcycle Club, who was affiliated with the Hells Angels, was arrested along with two other men who, according to the police investigation, had been in the car before the murder.
edges.context: A former Rodents MC member was affiliated with Hells Angels and involved in a murder investigation.

edges.evidence_quote: On 20 August 2001, two former members of the Brotherhood gang, Johan Ljung and Zlatko Radojkovic, were stabbed and beaten to death at the Hells Angels' clubhouse in Gunnilse outside Gothenburg.
edges.context: Hells Angels killed former members of the Brotherhood gang at their clubhouse.

edges.context: Bandidos are listed as a rival motorcycle gang of the Hells Angels.
